title: Sempervivella mucronata
intro: Sempervivella mucronata, also known as the Rosy Sempervivella, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family. The Rosy Sempervivella is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Sempervivella mucronata include saponins, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which may be used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.

It belongs to the genus Sempervivella, which is often considered a synonym of Sempervivum, a group of rosette-forming succulents. The family Crassulaceae includes over 1,400 species of plants, many of which are commonly known as jade plants or stonecrops. Sempervivella mucronata is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas.
